visual_base.visual_narrative_log: (1) The video opens at night showing the exterior of a Costco warehouse. A large white building with red 'COSTCO' branding is illuminated. A man in dark clothes walks near a parked scooter in the lower-left foreground. A cartoon character of Wreck-It Ralph is overlaid on the left. (2) The scene cuts to the interior of the warehouse, focusing on a stack of purple Kleenex tissue boxes. Large text overlays detail the discount. (3) Rapid visual cuts follow, showing various grocery items: vacuum-packed chicken thighs in a refrigerated display, followed by stacks of VIVA brand mixed nut boxes. (4) The sequence continues with fast cuts between different snack foods on warehouse shelves, including Cheetos, Cadina corn chips, and Witor's chocolates. Each shot is steady but very brief. (5) Moving through the aisles, the camera captures boxes of Lindt teddy bear chocolates, Richy oatmeal bites, and Maltesers. The lighting is consistently bright and clinical throughout the warehouse. (6) Further cuts show household and pantry items: Extra gum, Meiji Hello Panda biscuits, and various seaweed snacks, each appearing for less than two seconds. (7) The montage proceeds into fresh and frozen foods, showing boxes of mushrooms, kimchi, and prepared hotpot packs like pork belly with sauerkraut. (8) Dairy and chilled items are shown next, including Mascarpone tubs and chocolate mousse cups in clear plastic packaging. (9) The final segment of the montage covers beverages and household goods: Skippy peanut butter, Milo cereal, drip coffee, frozen beef noodles, and finally, a selection of alcoholic beverages including Yebisu beer and Johnnie Walker whiskey.

visual_human_presence.countingEvidence: 1 man visible in the intro scene walking by a moped.
visual_human_presence.mainCharacterDescription: A male pedestrian wearing a black T-shirt and dark shorts walking in front of the store at night.

visual_scene_and_style.backgroundDescribe: Costco exterior at night followed by numerous interior warehouse aisles with tall steel shelving, pallets of bulk-packaged products, and refrigerated display cases.

audio_base.audio_narrative_log: (1) High-tempo, upbeat electronic dance music begins immediately with a consistent percussive beat. A male narrator begins speaking very rapidly in Mandarin Chinese, his voice sounding highly energized and clear. He introduces the Costco weekly discount report with high vocal arousal and bright intonation. (2) As the music maintains its driving rhythm, the narrator's speech rate increases to a frantic pace. He starts listing items like tissues and chicken thighs, mentioning the original and discounted prices for each with virtually no pauses between words. (3) The rhythmic pattern continues without interruption. The narrator calls out a long sequence of snack and grocery brands, his tone remaining enthusiastic and loud, well-balanced above the synthesized background track. (4) The marathon-like delivery of product names and prices continues through the middle of the video. The electronic beat is steady and prominent, providing a sense of urgency that matches the rapid-fire vocal delivery. (5) As the list reaches chilled goods and finally alcoholic beverages, the narrator's energy remains peaked. He finishes the list with the same high-intensity pitch variability before the track stops abruptly, concluding the information-dense broadcast.

narrative_and_purpose.narrativeStructure: List-based: Intro -> Sequence of discounted products -> Ending
narrative_and_purpose.mainPurpose: The video serves as a rapid-fire information bulletin to inform Costco Taiwan members about specific sales and discounts available for the week of December 8-14, 2023. By using a fast-paced 'shopping list' format, it maximizes the amount of product information shared in a short time to entice shoppers to visit.

endorsement_analysis.endorsementCues: Detailed pricing, discount information, and clear focus on brand-name products within a specific retailer.

high_inference.freeDescribe: The video exemplifies the 'information overload' style of short-form social media marketing, relying on extreme speech speed and rapid cuts to maintain engagement.
